title: Ensure that 'TDE' is set to 'Enabled' on for applicable  database instance

input_row.title: Ensure that 'TDE' is set to 'Enabled' on for applicable  database instance
input_row.description: Enable Transparent Data Encryption on every RDS instance.
input_row.rationale: RDS Database transparent data encryption helps protect against the threat of malicious activity by performing real-time encryption and decryption of the database, associated backups, and log files at rest without requiring changes to the application.
input_row.audit: Using the management console: 1. Logon to RDS Console. 2. In the upper-left corner, select the region of the target instance. 3. Locate the target instance, and click the instance ID. 4. In the left-side navigation pane, click Data Security to go to the Security page. 5. Click the TDE tab. 6. Check the button TDE Status is Enabled.
input_row.remediation: Using the management console: 1. Logon to RDS Console. 2. In the upper-left corner, select the region of the target instance. 3. Locate the target instance, and click the instance ID to enter the Basic Information page. 4. In the left-side navigation pane, click Data Security to go to the Security page. 5. Click the TDE tab. 6. On the TDE tab, find TDE Status and click the switch next to Disabled. 7. In the displayed dialog box, choose automatically generated key or custom key, click Confirm. • Encrypt a table a. For RDS for MySQL, connect to the instance and run the following command to encrypt tables. alter table engine=innodb, block_format=encrypted b. For RDS for SQL Server, click Configure TDE, select the databases to encrypt, add them to the right, and click OK. • Decrypt data a. To decrypt a MySQL table encrypted by TDE, run the following command: alter table engine=innodb, block_format=default b. To decrypt a SQL Server table encrypted by TDE, click Configure TDE and move the database to the left.
input_row.additional_information: SQL Server 2008 R2, SQL Server 2008R2, SQL Server 2012 Enterprise Edition, SQL Server 2016 Enterprise Edition, SQL Server 2017 Enterprise Edition and MySQL 5.6/5.7/8.0 all support TED enablement. You have logged in with an Alibaba Cloud account rather than a RAM user account. KMS shall be activated. If KMS is not yet activated, you will be prompted to activate it when attempting to enable TDE.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The rationale clearly supports the technical ability to check this control.
- The audit field describes a step-by-step approach that can be translated into API/CLI calls.
- The context relates to a standard Alibaba Cloud service (RDS) and its encryption features.
- APIs exist for checking the TDE status in RDS instances.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_rds_instance_tde_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use the Alibaba Cloud API `DescribeDBInstances` to enumerate RDS instances across the required regions.
2. For each RDS instance, use the API call `DescribeSecurityGroup` (or equivalent) to check the TDE status specifically.
3. Validate the configuration property:
   - Check if `TDEStatus` is set to "Enabled". 
4. Pass/Fail criteria:
   - Pass: TDEStatus is "Enabled".
   - Fail: TDEStatus is "Disabled".
5. Command Example:
   - Run `aliyun rds DescribeDBInstances --RegionId <region_id>` to retrieve the list of instances.
   - For each instance, check the response for `TDEStatus` and confirm compliance based on the expected value "Enabled". 

This ensures that all applicable RDS instances are checked for TDE enablement automatically.
